description: Often considered the birthplace of coffee, Ethiopian Yirgacheffe is celebrated for its complex floral aromatics and bright, tea-like acidity. Washed processing highlights clarity and clean fruit notes.
Colombian Huila coffees benefit from ideal volcanic soil and two harvest seasons annually. Expect a smooth, balanced cup with sweet caramel notes and pleasant brightness.
Antigua's volcanic microclimate — surrounded by three volcanoes — produces coffees with exceptional body and a distinctive smoky-sweet complexity. Full-bodied with lingering chocolate finish.
Brazil is the world's largest coffee producer. Natural processing gives Minas Gerais beans a heavy body and rich chocolatey sweetness, ideal for espresso blends or those who prefer low acidity.
Kenyan SL28 and SL34 varietals from Nyeri are legendary for their intense, wine-like acidity and complex savory-fruit character. Bold, vibrant, and unforgettable.
Peruvian coffees from Cajamarca are often overlooked gems — clean, mild, and balanced with gentle sweetness. Popular for organic and fair-trade certifications.
Sumatra's unique wet-hulled (Giling Basah) processing creates the iconic heavy body and earthy, herbaceous complexity. A polarizing but beloved origin for fans of bold, savory coffee.
Yemen is where coffee culture began. Ancient heirloom varietals grown on terraced hillsides produce wild, winey, intensely complex cups unlike anything else in the world.
Tarrazú is Costa Rica's most celebrated coffee region. Honey-processed beans offer a middle ground between washed clarity and natural sweetness — juicy, clean, and approachable.
Home of the legendary Geisha varietal, Boquete is the crown jewel of specialty coffee. Expect extraordinary aromatic complexity — an almost tea-like elegance with floral and stone fruit notes.
